title: Kernel message on container shutdown.
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
When I stop container journald shows following message:
```bash
overlayfs: unrecognized mount option "volatile" or missing value
```
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman pull registry.fedoraproject.org/f29/httpd

2. podman run --rm --name fedora-httpd -dt  -p 8090:8080/tcp registry.fedoraproject.org/f29/httpd

3. podman stop fedora-httpd

**Describe the results you received:**
Message
```bash
kernel: overlayfs: unrecognized mount option "volatile" or missing value
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
No messages should be in kernel.  No volatile mount option set in storage.conf 

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.2.0-dev
API Version:  3.2.0-dev
Go Version:   go1.15.10
Git Commit:   9ba2e78cdcc28c15d72a76f6200a74e7ad6562b1-dirty
Built:        Mon Dec 31 16:00:00 1979
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

```


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Centos 7.x, Qemu VM.  I tried released version 3.1 and it produces the same kernel message.

comments.body:
How many times do you see that message in the log?

If it is just once then it is the test performed by containers/storage to verify whether volatile is supported.  Since it is not supported on the centos 7 kernel then it won't be used

After some cleaning it looks like messages appear after container stops.  Older versions of podman didn't trigger the same messages.

```console
Apr 27 00:13:51  Couldn't stat device /dev/char/10:200
Apr 27 00:13:51  2021-04-27 00:13:51.5655328 -0700 PDT m=+0.348990503 container init b6a8743d39fa61a7a9c2f85950856ccaea9fdbcbf6e0263d3fe895f779c3a870 (image=docker.io/library/httpd:latest, name=httpd)
Apr 27 00:13:51 podman[5123]: 2021-04-27 00:13:51.579013087 -0700 PDT m=+0.362470789 container start b6a8743d39fa61a7a9c2f85950856ccaea9fdbcbf6e0263d3fe895f779c3a870 (image=docker.io/library/httpd:latest, name=httpd)
Apr 27 00:13:51 conmon[5234]: AH00558: httpd: Could not reliably determine the server's fully qualified domain name, using 10.88.0.4. Set the 'ServerName' directive globally to suppress this message
Apr 27 00:13:51 conmon[5234]: AH00558: httpd: Could not reliably determine the server's fully qualified domain name, using 10.88.0.4. Set the 'ServerName' directive globally to suppress this message
Apr 27 00:13:51 conmon[5234]: [Tue Apr 27 07:13:51.623960 2021] [mpm_event:notice] [pid 1:tid 139803225207936] AH00489: Apache/2.4.46 (Unix) configured -- resuming normal operations
Apr 27 00:13:51 conmon[5234]: [Tue Apr 27 07:13:51.624153 2021] [core:notice] [pid 1:tid 139803225207936] AH00094: Command line: 'httpd -D FOREGROUND'
Apr 27 00:14:09 kernel: overlayfs: unrecognized mount option "volatile" or missing value
Apr 27 00:14:09 conmon[5234]: [Tue Apr 27 07:14:09.349462 2021] [mpm_event:notice] [pid 1:tid 139803225207936] AH00492: caught SIGWINCH, shutting down gracefully
Apr 27 00:14:10 kernel: overlayfs: unrecognized mount option "volatile" or missing value
Apr 27 00:14:10 podman[5353]: 2021-04-27 00:14:10.454556691 -0700 PDT m=+1.197140372 container stop b6a8743d39fa61a7a9c2f85950856ccaea9fdbcbf6e0263d3fe895f779c3a870 (image=docker.io/library/httpd:latest, name=httpd)
Apr 27 00:14:10 podman[5353]: 2021-04-27 00:14:10.486997307 -0700 PDT m=+1.229580953 container died b6a8743d39fa61a7a9c2f85950856ccaea9fdbcbf6e0263d3fe895f779c3a870 (image=docker.io/library/httpd:latest, name=httpd)
Apr 27 00:14:10 kernel: cni-podman0: port 1(vethd2d04f68) entered disabled state
Apr 27 00:14:10 kernel: device vethd2d04f68 left promiscuous mode
Apr 27 00:14:10 kernel: cni-podman0: port 1(vethd2d04f68) entered disabled state
Apr 27 00:14:10 podman[5397]: 2021-04-27 00:14:10.671749165 -0700 PDT m=+0.279814016 container remove b6a8743d39fa61a7a9c2f85950856ccaea9fdbcbf6e0263d3fe895f779c3a870 (image=docker.io/library/httpd:latest, name=httpd
```

When a podman container is stopped or exits, conmon gathers the exit code and then executes `podman container cleanup`.  This program cleans up the network stack and storage stack.  Since it is launching podman, podman loads container/storage.  When container/storage overlay driver is inited, it checks the for what options are available in the kernel for mounting.  It does this by actually setting up different mount points and mounting, and seeing what fails.  In this case it checks the volatile mount.  Since volatile mount is not supported on Centos 7, the kernel reports in it's logs that volatile is not supported.  This is nothing we can do to fix this.
@rhatdan and @giuseppe Thank you for a good explanation.

I wonder if this ticket can be reopened.  I installed podman  built with nix on Ubuntu 20.04. After configuring it, every podman command (running rooted)  hangs for few sec and then works but log of kernel has a new line.  It doesn't matter whether I run podman info,  podman rm container name, or podman run https://repo/xyz/name 
```
[Tue May 11 00:53:33 2021] overlayfs: unrecognized mount option "volatile" or missing value
[Tue May 11 00:53:37 2021] overlayfs: unrecognized mount option "volatile" or missing value
[Tue May 11 00:53:41 2021] overlayfs: unrecognized mount option "volatile" or missing value
[Tue May 11 00:53:54 2021] overlayfs: unrecognized mount option "volatile" or missing value
```
I have a configuration of storage set to mostly default values other than overlay for a driver.

```
Linux container2-la.xcastlabs.net 5.4.0-72-generic #80-Ubuntu SMP Mon Apr 12 17:35:00 UTC 2021 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

Per @giuseppe this is not a bug, but Podman searching for supported mount
options. The several second delay does not sound like it is expected.

So you saying that podman is looking for non-existent mount option on every command?  And you saying hanging for > 3 seconds on return is expected?  On every command?  I have 2.1 running on other servers with older kernel and it doesn't happen.   Also why would it look for a mount option not set in storage.conf?

@giuseppe is it intended?

NM, while messages in logs are annoying, delay problem was caused by loading CNI plugins.  Figured that out.  Apology for the noise.

I've also been bitten by this error message, on CentOS 8 Stream; I understand that you've got other things to address but any spurious error makes the troubleshooting of real errors more difficult, for admins as for any support person. I would kindly ask to reconsider the decision, it did cost me time to come to this issue and find out that the message isn't after all an error.
What error message are you talking about?  Just the debug messages that talk about volatile?

```
overlayfs: unrecognized mount option "volatile" or missing value
```
This message shows because storage code check support for volatile regardless if this option exists in storage.conf.  But some other options are only checked if they exist in the file. For example, I have:
```
mountopt = "nodev,metacopy=on"
```
If I put metacopy instead of metacopy=on, I would see
```
overlayfs: unrecognized mount option "metacopy" or missing value
```
However, if I have 
```
mountopt = "nodev"
```
Metacopy is not checked at all.


I am not sure if there is a reason to always check for volatile mount option if it doesn't exist among the mount options.
Perhaps in case volatile is enabled at the boot (kernel command ...)?

yes `volatile` doesn't have to specified in the mountopts but it is checked anyway and used for a certain class of containers that podman knows will be removed once they are terminated.

How are you running podman?  The result of the `volatile` check is cached and the mount attempted only once
I think the complaint is that every time they run podman it adds a line.
Correct, without any mean to know that it can be ignored. If the message can be cached, can't it be dropped as well?

I am still not getting any explanation but I wonder if seeing a lot of log entries is related to this.  I.e. even if I use 3.1.2 podman attempts to use volatile storage.
#### From 3.2.0 RC2 release notes.
Containers created with the --rm option now automatically use the volatile storage flag when available for their root filesystems, causing them not to write changes to disk as often as they will be removed at completion anyways. This should result in improved performance.

I upgraded to 3.2.0-RC2 and with new version of storage library the behavior is much more reasonable. It requests volatile on --rm and only one time on start.  BTW, the volatile overlay option was only added in kernel 5.10 so perhaps I get to enjoy some time next year after 22.04 released.  @giuseppe and @rhatdan Thank you for your work.
